title: Asemeia pohliana
intro: Asemeia pohliana, also known as Asemeia, is a plant species that belongs to the Malpighiaceae family and is native to Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Asemeia pohliana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Asemeia pohliana is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as to promote wound healing and immune system support.
